text: A risk you can name is a risk you can mitigate.

So, where does the room stand? Did Finance say which option they endorse?
They told me the dual-supplier plan would cost more, but they advised that it would protect us. So their recommendation is clear.
Good. And Operations -- did they say they had a shortlist?
Yes. They said they had drawn up three vendors, and they recommended that we ratify the top two this quarter. Nobody wanted to veto it.
So are we at consensus, or are we still in deadlock?
Almost there. One colleague said she would abstain, but the rest of us are ready to sign off. Let us not defer it -- I will finalize the paperwork tonight.

A decision deferred is a risk renewed.

Good morning. Before we vote, let me bring the room up to speed on where we stand. You will remember that last week the risk review flagged our single-supplier exposure as the threat we could not ignore. So the question on the table today is simple: which sourcing option do we endorse? Finance told me the dual-supplier plan would cost more up front, but they advised that it would protect us against a default. Operations said they had already drawn up a shortlist of three vendors, and they recommended that we ratify the top two this quarter. Legal asked whether the contracts could be signed before the end of the month, and the answer is yes, if we move now. Two people remain undecided, and one has said she will abstain. So here is where we are: we are close to consensus, but not there yet. I would ask everyone to weigh up the cost against the protection, and let us not leave this room in deadlock. A decision deferred is a risk renewed.

Hi team, here is my recap of the decision meeting, so everyone is aligned on what we agreed. After a long discussion, the group reached consensus on the dual-supplier plan. Marcus reminded us that the risk review had flagged the single-supplier exposure, and he asked which option we would endorse. Finance explained that the plan would cost more at first, but advised that it would shield us from a default. Operations told the room they had prepared a shortlist, and they proposed that we ratify the top two vendors this quarter. Nobody chose to veto the plan. One colleague decided to abstain, but the rest of us signed off, so the motion carried. We agreed not to defer the decision any further. I will finalize the paperwork and circulate the minutes by Friday. The room that decides together is the room that delivers together.

Marcus opens the decision meeting and reminds the room of the supplier risk. Finance reports that the dual-supplier plan costs more but protects against a default. Operations presents a shortlist of three vendors and recommends ratifying the top two. The group weighs up the cost against the protection and reaches consensus. Everyone signs off except one colleague, who abstains, and the motion carries.
